title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mr. Satterthwaite, an observant city man, is visiting the home of Colonel Melrose, a country gentleman and the county's chief police officer. While the two men are discussing horse racing in the Colonel's study, a phone call from Constable Curtis informs the Colonel that James DeWitt has been found murdered in his study. The Colonel invites Satterthwaite to accompany him to Alder Road to investigate the tricky case.

As they drive through the night, Satterthwaite recalls that the late Sir James was a stingy, angry man, while Lady DeWitt is a beautiful woman whom Satterthwaite describes as a "Renaissance type." The Colonel reveals that DeWitt was not poisoned but was beaten to death with a bronze statue. They also discuss Paul de Longueville, a man rumored to have been kicked out of the DeWitt residence a week prior after allegedly making advances toward Lady DeWitt.

Their journey is interrupted by a minor car collision at a British crossroads. The driver of the other vehicle is identified as Mr. Harry Queene. Satterthwaite becomes extremely excited upon recognizing Queene, noting that they had previously met at a hotel called "The Clock and Tweed." Satterthwaite tells the confused Colonel that Queene is a remarkable individual who previously solved the mysterious suicide of their friend, Derek Capel. Satterthwaite expresses a belief that their meeting at the crossroads was not an accident.
